page: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Dlsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 2 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache. System memory is limited to 8.0 GB, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities but limited multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is rated in the top 25% with a score of 2242.37, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches top-tier status at 14.78 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core stress-ng performance and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the bottom 25%, reflecting the physical core constraint of the 2-core hardware. Memory read bandwidth is average, measuring 335.78 GB/sec for small cached blocks, while large-block write bandwidth is weak at 23.19 GB/sec.

Given its low memory density and lack of local storage, the D4ls_v7 is a cost-efficient option for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. It is well-suited for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, and basic Redis operations, where it achieves an average of 8.2 million SET operations per second. However, it is poorly suited for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded parallel processing, or medium-to-large language model inference, as demonstrated by its poor 5.03 tokens per second generation speed on 7B models.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance from the Dlsv7 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, paired with 8.0 GB of system memory and no local storage. Benchmarks show strong single-core performance, with single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching the top 10% tier, though multi-core scaling and large-block memory writes remain weak. The instance features advanced CPU instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for single-threaded applications, lightweight web serving, and small-scale Redis deployments, but it is constrained for memory-heavy databases or complex multi-threaded workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v7 is a 4-vCPU compute-optimized server with 8.0 GB RAM and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for single-threaded tasks.
tagline: A low-memory, compute-focused virtual machine featuring high single-core clock speeds and advanced Intel matrix acceleration for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 480 MB L3 cache
4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory with no local storage
Strong single-core performance and top-tier single-threaded decompression
Advanced vector and matrix extensions including AVX-512 and AMX
Best suited for lightweight web serving, Redis, and single-threaded tasks
